title: clerodendrum phlomidis
intro: Clerodendrum phlomidis, also known as Phlomis, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to tropical regions in Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Phlomis include relief from fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Phlomis is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves and stems are also used in traditional remedies for their medicinal and antioxidant properties.
